stories.title: SpaceXAI releases Grok 4.5, which Elon describes as an ‘Opus-class model’
stories.simple_summary: Elon Musk's company released a new AI assistant called Grok 4.5 that they say works well but costs less money to use than similar AI tools.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use AI assistants, you might have cheaper options available soon — but this is mainly interesting to tech companies deciding which AI to buy.

stories.title: This startup thinks robotics is about to have its ChatGPT moment
stories.simple_summary: A startup thinks they can train robots to move and work better by using video game data instead of teaching them in the real world.
stories.why_it_matters: This could eventually make robots cheaper and easier to build, but it's still in early stages — nothing for small businesses to worry about right now.

stories.title: Opinion | Maybe Anthropic and OpenAI Are Not the Future of Artificial Intelligence
stories.simple_summary: An opinion article suggests that other AI companies besides Anthropic and OpenAI might be the real winners in the future.
stories.why_it_matters: This is someone's prediction about which tech companies will succeed — honestly, this doesn't affect you or your business decisions.

stories.title: Google Photos adds a new AI ‘Video Remix’ tool
stories.simple_summary: Google's photo app now has a new AI tool that can fix videos — like making dark videos brighter, changing backgrounds, or adding artistic effects.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use Google Photos, you'll soon be able to touch up videos without needing separate editing software — useful if you share family photos or business content.

stories.title: Why this CEO thinks video games make better training data than the internet
stories.simple_summary: A CEO believes that teaching robots using data from video games works better than teaching them from information on the internet.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly important to engineers building robots — not something you need to know about unless you're considering buying robotic equipment.

stories.title: Meta wants its AI glasses to seem less creepy. Its AI strategy says otherwise.
stories.simple_summary: Meta is adding a feature to its AI glasses so they won't secretly record people, but the company is also collecting more personal data for its AI in other ways.
stories.why_it_matters: If you're thinking about buying Meta's AI glasses, know that the company is collecting personal information — you should decide if you're comfortable with that.

stories.title: OpenAI releases new voice models for more natural live conversations
stories.simple_summary: OpenAI released new voice tools for their AI that can listen and talk at the same time, which is good for real-time translation between languages.
stories.why_it_matters: This could eventually make real-time translation easier, but it's mainly being tested by early users right now.

stories.title: ChatGPT’s upgraded voice mode is better at shutting up
stories.simple_summary: OpenAI improved their ChatGPT voice feature so it listens better and doesn't interrupt you as much — it's supposed to feel more like talking to a real person.
stories.why_it_matters: If you talk to ChatGPT using your voice, conversations will feel more natural now.

stories.title: Prime Intellect raises $130M Series A to help enterprises build their own AI agents
stories.simple_summary: A company raised $130 million to help other businesses build their own AI assistants without depending on big AI companies like OpenAI.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors — it doesn't change what's available to you right now.

stories.title: These AI startups are growing  revenue at faster and faster rates
stories.simple_summary: Several AI startups are making money faster and faster, according to reports.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly interesting to investors and people tracking tech industry trends — it doesn't affect your daily life.

stories.title: Datacentres are a ticking time bomb. We must make sure AI’s benefits outweigh the costs | Nicki Hutley
stories.simple_summary: An opinion piece warns that the huge computer buildings that power AI use a lot of electricity and could cause problems, so we need to make sure AI's benefits are worth the cost.
stories.why_it_matters: This raises important questions about whether AI's benefits are worth its environmental cost — it's a fair concern, but mostly a discussion for policymakers.

stories.title: Nvidia’s $1 Trillion Slide Sends Valuation to Pre-AI Boom Levels
stories.simple_summary: Nvidia, the company that makes computer chips for AI, saw its stock price drop significantly in recent weeks.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to people who own Nvidia stock — if you don't own tech company stock, this doesn't affect you directly.
